laws.title: Public Law 23-172 | Congressional Military Service Act
laws.status_basis: The source text is a repealing and substituting military-service enactment. The chronology report ties its operative targets to 10 U.S.C. 12203 and 10 U.S.C. 151, but the later validation path treats the law as historical only, so no live Code text is authorized.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and immediate-effective-date language.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the opening clauses as history only.
provisions.evidence: The recovered enactment begins with standard title, severability, and effective-date clauses.
provisions.notes: No current Code text is created from these clauses.

provisions.text_summary: Amends PL 18-140 section 304(a) to revise enlistment and commissioning rules.
provisions.exact_change: Do not elevate this substituted text into current Code text.
provisions.evidence: Section 4(1) expressly rewrites section 304(a) of Public Law 18-140.
provisions.notes: This is a repealed or superseded amendment path.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 10 U.S.C. 12204 and rewrites 10 U.S.C. 12203.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve only the repeal history; do not treat the withdrawn wording as current law.
provisions.evidence: Chronology overlaps show the 10 U.S.C. 12203 and 12204 targets as part of this enactment's amendment path.
provisions.notes: The repeal history belongs in a historical note only.

provisions.text_summary: Rewrites 10 U.S.C. 151 and adds uncodified protections and limitations.
provisions.exact_change: Keep only the enactment history; do not create live Title 10 text from the repealed act.
provisions.evidence: The chronology report associates this law with 10 U.S.C. 151.
provisions.notes: The uncodified directions are not current Code text.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML preserves the enactment as history rather than live Code text, which matches the repealed disposition in both the branch and origin/main treatment.

recommended_actions: Retain the enactment only as a historical note.
Do not promote the substituted or repealed text into current Code sections.
Remove the Trello link if it remains attached.

laws.title: Public Law 24-173 | Federal Enhancement Against Foreign Intrusion Act
laws.status_basis: The recovered source is a general and permanent foreign-affairs statute with no clean positive-law rewrite target. It is best treated as a statutory note anchored to the nearest subject-matter code location rather than as free-standing operative text.

provisions.text_summary: Bars federal employees from holding positions in foreign governments.

provisions.evidence: The section prohibits federal employees from holding foreign-government positions.
provisions.notes: No separate Code section is determinable from the source.

provisions.text_summary: Bars elected officials from foreign-government or foreign-military employment.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as statutory-note material only.
provisions.evidence: The section bars elected officials from foreign-government or foreign-military employment.
provisions.notes: The act remains note material rather than a positive-law section rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Extends the foreign-position bar to Senate-confirmed officials.

laws.title: Public Law 24-178 | Equality in Employment Act
laws.status_basis: The source expressly amends 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a) to add gender identity and sexual orientation. The enacted change is substantive and should be carried in Title 42, with the remainder retained as note material.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, preamble, and findings.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the framing clauses as note material only.
provisions.evidence: The opening sections introduce the act and contain findings rather than current Code text.
provisions.notes: These are ancillary clauses.

provisions.text_summary: Amends 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a) to add gender identity and sexual orientation.
provisions.exact_change: Add gender identity and sexual orientation to the protected characteristics in subsection (a).
provisions.evidence: Section 6(a) expressly directs amendment of 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a).
provisions.notes: The substantive amendment is already reflected in the current code path.

provisions.text_summary: EEOC and Labor implementation directives.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the agency directives as concise note material only.
provisions.evidence: The source directs agency enforcement and rulemaking.
provisions.notes: These directives are not a positive-law rewrite target.

provisions.text_summary: Conclusion and sense language.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the closing language as history only.
provisions.evidence: The closing section is a concluding sense statement rather than positive-law text.
provisions.notes: No separate code text should be derived from it.

current_implementation.detail: The Title 42 amendment is present, but the codification note is still mis-anchored to Title 29 and carries a full-text dump that should be removed.

recommended_actions: Keep the Title 42 amendment as applied text.
Move or replace the note at the correct Title 42 anchor.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from the note.
Retain the EEOC and Labor directives as concise statutory notes.

laws.title: Public Law 25-180 | Sanction Repealment Act
laws.status_basis: The source is a repealer statute. It expressly repeals Public Law 9-55 in full and declares the Iran sanctions regime null and void, so the codification treatment must preserve repeal-marking at the original law home and keep the rest as note material.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the opening clauses as concise note material only.
provisions.evidence: The opening clauses identify the repealing statute and its purpose.
provisions.notes: These clauses do not require live Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals PL 9-55 in its entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Record repeal-marking at the original law's home.
provisions.evidence: Section 3(b) expressly repeals Public Law 9-55 in its entirety.
provisions.notes: This is the key operative repeal path.

provisions.text_summary: Declares sanctions on Iran null and void.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the sanctions-nullification language as a short note only.
provisions.evidence: Section 3(c) declares all sanctions on Iran null and void.
provisions.notes: This is note material, not a positive-law rewrite target.

provisions.text_summary: Severability and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Keep standard severability and effective-date clauses as note material.
provisions.evidence: The source includes severability and immediate-effect language.
provisions.notes: No additional code placement is justified.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note is a reasonable foreign-relations anchor, but the repeal of PL 9-55 still needs to be recorded at the original Code home.

recommended_actions: Record the PL 9-55 repeal as a repeal-marking at its original Code home.
Keep the sanctions-nullification language as a concise statutory note.
Remove the Trello link.
